Mtn To Compel Responses To Req For Production Of Docs
Off Calendar Jan-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: BRADFORD A. ROSENBLATT, ESQ., 415.840.7177 X210, FOR PLAINTIFF CLARCEEN CALDWELL; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WALTER SCOTT's motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents is denied. As to Request for Production nos. 18 - 20, pursuant to which defendants seek retainer agreements, these agreements are privileged pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code 6068(e) and 6169 and Evid. Code 952. Plaintiffs have not waived any privilege. As to Request for Production nos. 23 - 25, Plaintiffs have not put their financial information at issue. Further, it is protected by the constitutional right to privacy (Cal. Const. Art. 1, 1), and, in any event, moving parties have not shown how even under a balancing test such documents should be compelled here. Monetary sanctions were not requested, and none are rewarded. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
